title: Sinners Production Design: Folklore, History, and Spirituality
text: Sinners is a 2025 American horror film produced, written, and directed by Ryan Coogler, set in 1932 in the Mississippi Delta, starring Michael B. Jordan in dual roles as criminal twin brothers who return to their hometown in the Jim Crow South, where they are confronted by a supernatural evil. The film features an Irish-immigrant vampire character named Remmick. Oscar-winning production designer Hannah Beachler crafted the film's Clarksdale, Mississippi through arduous research into the period's real-life architectural details. Beachler made a point of highlighting the economic hardships Black residents faced in the Jim Crow era, with starkly contrasted scenes of the Black and white sides of town offering glimpses into the station of Black Americans of that era in the Delta. The production design work showcases the intersection of historical accuracy and supernatural storytelling, creating a world that feels authentic while embracing the film's horror elements.
